ref: I will give you and them the land in which you are now a foreigner. I will give the whole land of Canaan to your family forever, and I will be their God.
text: Na mebava azɛlɛ ne mɔɔ ɛlɛkpɔsakpɔsa zolɛ la meamaa wɔ nee ɛ bo zo amra. Bɛbava Keena azɛlɛ ne kɔsɔɔti bɛayɛ bɛ ɛdeɛ, na mebayɛ bɛ Nyamenle.”
translated: Mebava said that they came to inquire together with them to do the work. Bebava Keena said that they were at the market, and they are from Nyamenle.

ref: You didn't have much before I came, but the Lord has blessed everything I have ever done for you. Now it's time for me to start looking out for my own family.
text: Ɔluakɛ ɛnee wɔ nyɛmoa le ekyi bie yɛɛ membale a, kɛkala bɛ nwo ɛzo dɔɔnwo kpalɛ, yɛɛ medame me ti, Ɛdɛnkɛma ɛyila wɔ wɔ debie biala anu. Na kɛkala menea a akee yedwu mekɛ mɔɔ ɔwɔ kɛ medwenle me sua nu amra anwo a.”
translated: Let us all come together as one people, let us unite our strength and work together, my friend, as the saying goes, 'United we stand, divided we fall.' And let us not forget that by helping each other, we strengthen our family bonds and ensure a better future for all of us.

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
text: Na mɔɔ nrenya ne nwunle kɛ yeangola yeangomo Gyekɔbo anwo zo la, ɔvale ɔ sa ɔhanle Gyekɔbo ɛzɔlɛ, na Gyekɔbo adwongu huanenle wɔ ɛfutulɛ ne anu.
translated: And so the path and the people in Yeangola Yeangomo Gyekɔbo went there, and Gyekɔbo declared that they recognized Gyekɔbo's dwelling, and Gyekɔbo remained to guard them in Efutulɛ with the others.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Yemɔti Nyamenle bɔle sonla kɛ ɔdaye ye ɛsaso la; ɔdaye Nyamenle mumua ne ye ɛsaso a ɔnleanle ɔbɔle sonla a. Ɔbɔle bɛ nrenya nee raalɛ.
translated: Yemoti Nyamenle brought the offering to his day, and on that day Nyamenle prepared the saso that he had brought along with the bɔle offering. The bɔle is the one called raalɛ.

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Yemɔti fa gɔfa baka bobɔ ɛlɛnemgbole; kyɛ nuhua ekpumgbunli ekpumgbunli na fa bilewu ka nuhua nee nwolɛ amuala.
translated: Yemoti went to fetch the baka from Elnemgbole; and the nuhua, ekpumgbunli, ekpumgbunli, then took the bilewu from the nuhua to nwolɛ Amuala.

translated: And the people are working hard together in their place to make a journey. Keena, the people who came, are here, and they are from Nyamenle.

ref: You didn't have much before I came, but the Lord has blessed everything I have ever done for you. Now it's time for me to start looking out for my own family.
text: Ɔluakɛ ɛnee wɔ nyɛmoa le ekyi bie yɛɛ membale a, kɛkala bɛ nwo ɛzo dɔɔnwo kpalɛ, yɛɛ medame me ti, Ɛdɛnkɛma ɛyila wɔ wɔ debie biala anu. Na kɛkala menea a akee yedwu mekɛ mɔɔ ɔwɔ kɛ medwenle me sua nu amra anwo a.”
translated: Oluake, when the members gather in the village, the elders will come together to discuss, the mother's side is also present, Edenkema has come to lead the ceremony. And the gathering is for the purpose of resolving the dispute between the two villages.

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
text: Na mɔɔ nrenya ne nwunle kɛ yeangola yeangomo Gyekɔbo anwo zo la, ɔvale ɔ sa ɔhanle Gyekɔbo ɛzɔlɛ, na Gyekɔbo adwongu huanenle wɔ ɛfutulɛ ne anu.
translated: And so the path and the journey to Yeangola Yeangomo, Gyekɔbo went there to see, and Gyekɔbo, upon arriving at the place, they went to Efutulɛ with them.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Yemɔti Nyamenle bɔle sonla kɛ ɔdaye ye ɛsaso la; ɔdaye Nyamenle mumua ne ye ɛsaso a ɔnleanle ɔbɔle sonla a. Ɔbɔle bɛ nrenya nee raalɛ.
translated: Yemoti Nyamenle brought the path to him/her for the story; he/she Nyamenle went with the story that he/she followed the path to their home.

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Yemɔti fa gɔfa baka bobɔ ɛlɛnemgbole; kyɛ nuhua ekpumgbunli ekpumgbunli na fa bilewu ka nuhua nee nwolɛ amuala.
translated: Yemoti goes to the ancestral house with offerings, repeatedly bringing them to ask the people for the amuala.

ref: I will give you and them the land in which you are now a foreigner. I will give the whole land of Canaan to your family forever, and I will be their God.
text: Na mebava azɛlɛ ne mɔɔ ɛlɛkpɔsakpɔsa zolɛ la meamaa wɔ nee ɛ bo zo amra. Bɛbava Keena azɛlɛ ne kɔsɔɔti bɛayɛ bɛ ɛdeɛ, na mebayɛ bɛ Nyamenle.”
translated: So the person goes to inquire, to know and confirm the path he is taking. The person Keena, when asked, says it is so, and they are in Nyamenle.

ref: You didn't have much before I came, but the Lord has blessed everything I have ever done for you. Now it's time for me to start looking out for my own family.
text: Ɔluakɛ ɛnee wɔ nyɛmoa le ekyi bie yɛɛ membale a, kɛkala bɛ nwo ɛzo dɔɔnwo kpalɛ, yɛɛ medame me ti, Ɛdɛnkɛma ɛyila wɔ wɔ debie biala anu. Na kɛkala menea a akee yedwu mekɛ mɔɔ ɔwɔ kɛ medwenle me sua nu amra anwo a.”
translated: Oluake says that when we gather here in the village, we must come with our hearts united, and the elders have decreed that Edenkema’s words spoken here hold power. And when we assemble, let everyone who knows the truth speak openly before the chief so that we can resolve this matter properly.

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
text: Na mɔɔ nrenya ne nwunle kɛ yeangola yeangomo Gyekɔbo anwo zo la, ɔvale ɔ sa ɔhanle Gyekɔbo ɛzɔlɛ, na Gyekɔbo adwongu huanenle wɔ ɛfutulɛ ne anu.
translated: Then, the path and the journey to Yeangola Yeangomo, Gyekɔbo's place, were difficult, and Gyekɔbo had to endure hardships in the forest with them.
